# Mexico City city guide

## Overview

Mexico City (Ciudad de Mexico, CDMX) is one of the world's great megacities: 21 million people, thousands of years of history, and a food scene that ranges from $1 street tacos to world-ranked fine dining. Every neighborhood has its own personality, the art and architecture are staggering, and the warmth of the people makes everything better.

**Best time to visit**: Mar-May for warm, dry weather. Oct-Nov for Dia de los Muertos. Jun-Sep is rainy season (afternoon showers).
**Currency**: Mexican Peso (MXN). 17 MXN ≈ 1 USD.
**Language tip**: Spanish is essential. English is limited outside tourist areas. "Por favor" and "gracias" are your foundation.

## Neighborhoods

| Neighborhood | Vibe | Best for | Transit |
|---|---|---|---|
| Roma Norte | Trendy, tree-lined | Restaurants, cafes, galleries, nightlife | Metro Insurgentes or Chapultepec |
| Condesa | Chic, leafy | Art Deco architecture, parks, brunch, bars | Metro Chapultepec or Chilpancingo |
| Centro Historico | Grand, chaotic | Zocalo, Palacio de Bellas Artes, street food | Metro Zocalo |
| Coyoacan | Bohemian, colorful | Frida Kahlo Museum, markets, churros | Metro Coyoacan |
| Polanco | Upscale, international | Fine dining, luxury shopping, Chapultepec | Metro Polanco |
| San Rafael | Emerging, authentic | Local food, Art Deco theaters, less touristy | Metro San Cosme |
| Xochimilco | Festive, unique | Trajinera boat rides, floating gardens | Metro/light rail Xochimilco |
| Santa Fe | Modern, business | Malls, corporate offices, contemporary architecture | Bus/Uber from Polanco |

## Food

| Name | Category | Neighborhood | Budget | Description | Must-order |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Tacos El Califa de Leon | Tacos | Centro/Multiple | $ | Michelin-starred street tacos. Bistec and costilla on a hot griddle, served on fresh tortillas. Simple perfection. | Taco de bistec, taco de costilla |
| Pujol | Fine dining | Polanco | $$$ | Enrique Olvera's legendary restaurant. The mole madre (two moles, 1500+ days aging) is iconic. Book months ahead. | Mole madre, taco omakase |
| El Moro Churreria | Churros | Centro Historico | $ | Churros and hot chocolate since 1935. Open 24 hours, neon-lit, and absolutely perfect at 2 AM. | Churros with chocolate espeso |
| Mercado Roma | Food hall | Roma Norte | $-$$ | A curated food market with tacos, ramen, craft beer, and mezcal. Rooftop bar with city views. | Tacos, craft mezcal, seasonal specials |
| Contramar | Seafood | Roma Norte | $$-$$$ | The tuna tostadas and red-and-green grilled fish are the most Instagrammed dishes in CDMX. Book ahead. | Tostadas de atun, pescado a la talla |
| Los Cocuyos | Tacos | Centro Historico | $ | Late-night taco stand with suadero (brisket) and longaniza tacos by the glow of a bare light bulb. | Suadero tacos, longaniza |
| Rosetta | Italian-Mexican | Roma Norte | $$$ | Elena Reygadas' beautiful restaurant in a Roma mansion. Italian techniques with Mexican ingredients. | Ricotta stuffed squash blossoms, pasta |
| El Hidalguense | Barbacoa | Multiple | $ | Weekend barbacoa (slow-cooked lamb) wrapped in maguey leaves. The consomme is liquid gold. | Barbacoa with consomme, salsa verde |
| Cafe de Tacuba | Traditional Mexican | Centro Historico | $$ | Gorgeous tiled restaurant serving classic Mexican dishes since 1912. Enchiladas and mole are benchmark. | Enchiladas suizas, mole poblano |
| Expendio de Maiz | Traditional | Centro | $$ | Corn-focused restaurant celebrating indigenous Mexican cuisine. Every tortilla is nixtamalized in-house. | Blue corn quesadillas, seasonal specials |

## Landmarks and activities

| Name | Category | Neighborhood | Budget | Time needed | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Museo Frida Kahlo (Casa Azul) | Museum | Coyoacan | $$ | 2-3 hours | The Blue House where Frida lived and worked. Her art, personal belongings, and gardens. Book timed tickets online. |
| Palacio de Bellas Artes | Art/Architecture | Centro Historico | $ | 1-2 hours | Art Deco and Art Nouveau masterpiece. Diego Rivera and Siqueiros murals inside. Check for ballet or concert schedules. |
| Templo Mayor | History | Centro Historico | $$ | 2 hours | Aztec temple ruins in the heart of the city, right next to the cathedral. The museum has incredible artifacts. |
| Chapultepec Castle (Castillo de Chapultepec) | History/Views | Chapultepec | $ | 2-3 hours | The only royal castle in the Americas. Panoramic city views from the terrace. Walk through Chapultepec Park to reach it. |
| Xochimilco Floating Gardens | Culture | Xochimilco | $$ | 3-4 hours | Colorful trajinera boats through ancient Aztec canals. Mariachis and food vendors float alongside. Go on weekends. |
| Museo Nacional de Antropologia | Museum | Chapultepec | $$ | 3-4 hours | One of the world's great museums. The Aztec Sun Stone, Maya artifacts, and indigenous cultures of Mexico. |
| Coyoacan Market and Square | Culture | Coyoacan | Free | 2-3 hours | Wander the colorful colonial neighborhood. Market stalls with tostadas, artisans, and the Coyoacan church. |
| Teotihuacan Pyramids | Day trip | Outside CDMX | $$ | Full day | Pyramid of the Sun, Pyramid of the Moon, Avenue of the Dead. An hour from the city. Go early, bring sun protection. |
| Zocalo (Plaza de la Constitucion) | Culture | Centro Historico | Free | 1 hour | One of the largest public squares in the world. Metropolitan Cathedral, National Palace, ancient Aztec ruins. |
| Lucha Libre at Arena Mexico | Entertainment | Centro | $-$$ | 3 hours | Friday night wrestling with masks, capes, and crowd energy you won't believe. Get floor seats for the full experience. |

## Nightlife

| Name | Category | Neighborhood | Budget | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Alvaro Obregon Bar Strip | Bars | Roma Norte | $-$$ | The main avenue in Roma Norte lined with bars, from mezcalerias to craft cocktail spots. |
| Salon Tenampa | Cantina | Centro | $$ | Legendary mariachi cantina in Plaza Garibaldi since 1925. Hire a mariachi table-side with tequila. |
| Departamento | Speakeasy | Condesa | $$ | Hidden cocktail bar behind an unmarked door. Mezcal cocktails, intimate space, excellent bartenders. |
| Patrick Miller | Dance club | San Rafael | $ | Saturday night dance-off parties. Locals dance in circles, the energy is insane. Pure CDMX. |
| Limantour | Cocktail bar | Roma Norte | $$ | Ranked among the world's best bars. Mexican-inspired cocktails that are innovative and beautifully crafted. |

## Transit tips

- **Metro**: Massive 12-line system, incredibly cheap (5 MXN per ride). Efficient but very crowded during rush hour.
- **Metrobus**: Bus rapid transit on dedicated lanes. Line 1 runs Insurgentes, the main north-south artery. Rechargeable card.
- **Uber/DiDi**: Very affordable and the preferred way to get around for most visitors. Use over taxis.
- **Walking**: Roma, Condesa, and Centro are very walkable. The tree-lined streets of Roma Norte are a highlight.
- **Airport**: Benito Juarez International (MEX) is close to the city. Uber or authorized taxi from the terminal.
- **Pro tip**: Download the CDMX Metro app. Also, women and children have dedicated cars during rush hour.
- **Altitude**: CDMX is at 2,240 meters. You may feel short of breath the first day or two. Stay hydrated.

## Useful Spanish phrases

| English | Spanish | Romanization |
|---|---|---|
| Hello | Hola | OH-lah |
| Thank you | Gracias | GRAH-see-as |
| Please | Por favor | por fa-VOR |
| The bill please | La cuenta, por favor | lah KWEN-tah por fa-VOR |
| Delicious! | Que rico! | keh REE-ko |
| How much? | Cuanto cuesta? | KWAN-toh KWES-tah |
| Cheers! | Salud! | sa-LOOD |
| Where is...? | Donde esta...? | DON-deh es-TAH |
